summary: Cross-validated Phase 002 CONTAINS edge population and found the current indexer emits only class-to-method containment, not symbol-to-file/module chains across all supported languages.

label: CONTAINS is not a symbol-to-file/module chain
CONTAINS is not emitted for all supported languages
Go and Rust WASMs are present but not runtime-supported languages
Nested classes can break upward containment selection
Python decorators do not affect CONTAINS, but decorator edges are separate
Anonymous functions inside classes and export-default classes are sparse trace cases
TypeScript namespace, Go interface methods, and Rust impl blocks are out of current parser scope
The fq_name prefix fallback is specified but not robust enough as written
Empty CONTAINS chains are specified, but implementation tests must enforce the shape
